prompt: Refactor /Users/mikkelfreltoftkrogsholm/Projekter/dev-skills/agent-readable-code/scripts/fixtures/bad_py/utils.py so an AI agent can maintain it. Produce problems.md, refactored.py, rationale.md.

assertions.text: problems.md explicitly names 'utils.py' as a dumping-ground / generic filename problem
problems.md calls out the generic class name 'Manager' as a naming problem
problems.md identifies __getattr__ / dynamic dispatch as a problem for AI agents
problems.md flags the use of eval() as an anti-pattern
problems.md identifies the deep inheritance chain

refactored.py uses @dataclass(slots=True) or @dataclass(frozen=True) to prevent ad-hoc attribute fabrication (v1.3 guidance)
rationale.md or problems.md explains why slots/__all__ matters for agents (hallucinated attributes, import of internal helpers) — the v1.3 framing
